function_explanation: Performs a 2D FFT operation with proper center-shifting and normalization on the input tensor.
function_docstring:
Performs a 2D Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) on a batched tensor with support for
applying orthogonal normalization and center-shifting in frequency domain executions.

Args:
    x (torch.Tensor): A complex-valued tensor of shape (..., H, W) representing the spatial
domain image or signal data on which the 2D FFT is to be performed. The inner most two dimensions (H, W)
are processed for FFTs.

Returns:
    torch.Tensor: A complex-valued tensor of the same shape as the input (..., H, W)
representing the frequency domain representation of the input data after FFT processing.
usage_example:
import torch

# Create a random complex tensor of shape (4, 32, 32)
x = torch.randn(4, 32, 32, dtype=torch.complex64)

# Apply the 2D FFT transformation on the tensor
result = mri_fft(x)

# Verify the shape
assert result.shape == x.shape

# Use the transformed frequency domain result
print(result)
